# VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0013 - QUIC RFC 9000 NEW_CONNECTION_ID Frame Codec Verification

## Scope

This verification covers the supported NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame-codec floor: accepting frame type 0x18, preserving Sequence Number and Retire Prior To variable-length integer values, encoding the one-byte connection ID length, preserving the declared connection ID and 16-byte stateless reset token, rejecting invalid lengths and truncation, and rejecting Retire Prior To values greater than Sequence Number.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0002
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0003
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0004
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0005
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0006
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0007
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0008
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0010
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0011
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0012
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0013
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0019
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P15-0020

## Verification Method

Requirement-home execution, deterministic frame-codec fuzz coverage, BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation, reciprocal trace inspection, and SpecTrace render/check for the bounded NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame-codec slice.

## Preconditions

- The frame codec receives a complete contiguous NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame payload.
- The caller treats a false codec return as malformed or unsupported input and owns connection-close emission outside this frame-local parser.
- The caller owns peer connection-ID lifecycle state outside the frame-codec entry point.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the focused RFC 9000 NEW_CONNECTION_ID requirement-home tests for S19P15-0002, S19P15-0008, S19P15-0011, and S19P15-0019.
- Confirm deterministic frame-codec fuzz coverage remains linked for representative NEW_CONNECTION_ID round-trips and truncation rejection.
- Run `QuicFrameCodecBenchmarks` with the Dry job.
- Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
- Confirm the focused artifact set is reciprocal only for the intended S19P15 codec requirements.
- Confirm that no peer CID registry, migration routing, stateless-reset response, public API, or interop migration claim is introduced.

## Expected Result

Focused tests prove positive, negative, and edge NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame-codec behavior, deterministic fuzz coverage exercises representative valid shapes and truncation rejection, and the BenchmarkDotNet Dry suite executes representative parse and format hot-path cases without treating Dry output as a stable performance baseline.

## Status

Passed locally on 2026-04-25. Focused NEW_CONNECTION_ID requirement-home tests passed 14/14, and the updated QuicFrameCodecBenchmarks Dry suite executed all 12 frame-codec cases including NEW_CONNECTION_ID parse and format. Dry benchmark results are harness evidence only and are not treated as stable performance baselines.

## Fuzz And Benchmark Scope

The deterministic requirement-home fuzz proof covers representative NEW_CONNECTION_ID round-trips plus truncation rejection through the frame-codec entry point. The permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite covers NEW_CONNECTION_ID parsing and formatting alongside the existing frame-codec cases; Dry validation is harness evidence only and is not treated as a stable performance baseline.
